summary: Audited radar/config.py, radar/models.py, radar/scheduling/scheduler.py, radar/ladder.py, radar/scorer.py, and radar/synthetic.py for config validation gaps, model consistency, scheduler reliability, and edge cases. CRITICAL ISSUES: 1. config.py:251 - HIGH - RadarSettings defines vader_weight/textblob_weight fields but Settings already has sentiment_vader_weight/sentiment_textblob_weight. This creates dual validation that can diverge. Fix: Remove RadarSettings or unify field names. 2. config.py:186-207 - MEDIUM - validate_smtp_when_email_enabled and validate_reddit_when_enabled only validate credentials exist, not their format. Fix: Add regex validation for email format, check that client IDs aren't placeholder strings. 3. models.py:33-36 - MEDIUM - _sha256_url normalizes URLs by lowercasing and stripping trailing slash, but doesn't handle query params or fragments. Different query strings will produce different hashes for the same resource. Fix: Parse URL and normalize components consistently. 4. models.py:69-79 - LOW - compute_url_hash validator accesses info.data which may be AttributeError if info is None. Current try/except catches this but defaults to empty string silently. Fix: Log validation failures. 5. models.py:81-105 - MEDIUM - model_post_init synchronizes aliases bidirectionally (e.g., score <-> upvotes). If both are set to different values, last write wins with no warning. Fix: Validate alias consistency or pick a canonical field. 6. models.py:159-168 - LOW - DailyReport.model_post_init sets is_partial=True when entry_count < 5, but this hardcodes the fallback ladder TARGET. Fix: Accept TARGET as parameter or reference FallbackLadder.TARGET. 7. scheduler.py:47-49, 69-71, 91-93 - HIGH - Cron parsing splits on space and expects exactly 5 parts, but doesn't validate cron syntax. Invalid cron like "60 * * * *" will silently create a broken trigger. Fix: Use croniter or validate ranges. 8. scheduler.py:39-40 - MEDIUM - stop() calls shutdown(wait=False), which may orphan running jobs if called during job execution. Fix: Use wait=True with timeout to allow jobs to complete gracefully. 9. scheduler.py:112-146 - MEDIUM - Job handlers catch all exceptions and log them, but don't implement any retry logic or alerting. A permanent failure (e.g., database corruption) will fail silently every schedule. Fix: Add circuit breaker or dead-letter alerting. 10. ladder.py:20-27 - LOW - get_five() checks len(result) >= TARGET but doesn't handle empty _fetch_live() returning None. Current code works because _fetch_live returns list, but type hint says List[Dict] allows None. Fix: Explicitly validate return types. 11. ladder.py:42-48 - MEDIUM - archive-30d deduplication only checks existing_urls from result, not from archive-7d tier. If a post appears in both archive tiers, it could be duplicated. Fix: Track all seen URLs across all tiers. 12. ladder.py:54-60 - LOW - Posts with unrecognized source_tier values get set to "partial". This means typos in tier names will silently downgrade provenance. Fix: Validate tier values against enum. 13. scorer.py:28-72 - LOW - rank() extracts sentiment_score and maintainer_signals from dicts, but doesn't validate types. If post contains {"sentiment_score": "high"}, this will raise TypeError. Fix: Add type guards and coercion. 14. scorer.py:51-54 - MEDIUM - Maintainer boost is 1.25 if n_signals >= 2, else 1.0. This threshold is hardcoded and not configurable. Fix: Add maintainer_boost_threshold parameter. 15. synthetic.py:132-149 - LOW - _pick_keyword strips regex metacharacters to create readable text, but the replacements are fragile and could produce [truncated]
